extraction.one_line_summary: MATTHEWS Michael attacked from Van Avermaet's wheel on final climb in Rodez, soloing to victory while ARU lost 24 seconds and yellow jersey through catastrophic positioning failure.
tactical_patterns: Sunweb/BMC pre-race collaboration neutralized breakaway by splitting chase burden, preventing usual team isolation problem in stage control.
Matthews attacked 500m+ out on final climb after stalking Van Avermaet's wheel through technical finale, exploiting longer distance sprint range.
Froome/Sky gained 24 seconds on ARU without attacking, forcing time gaps through tempo control when ARU lost all teammates before finale.
Sky anticipates Landa teammates will now suppress attacks to protect Froome's yellow, changing team dynamics from previous aggressive stage racing.

rider_signals.ARU Fabio: negative: isolated at back of peloton entering finale with no teammates, catastrophic positioning cost 24 seconds and yellow on non-selective stage
rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: delivered long-range sprint attack after full day's team chase work, suggesting exceptional diesel-sprinter engine for hilly finishes
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: Sky reclaimed yellow through opportunistic positioning when rival team collapsed, expects peloton control will suppress Landa's internal attacks
rider_signals.LANDA Mikel: neutral: sits 1:36 down but Froome expects Sky will now suppress his attacks to defend yellow rather than allow freedom
rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: negative: dropped with three teammates on moderate terrain, signaling vulnerability on any undulating stage despite green jersey lead
